IPsec ESP Outbound (gxcr/tokens/esp_ip4v_tunnel_obound.h)
[IPsec API (gxcr/ipsec.h)]

Detailed Description

The ESP IPv4 Tunneling Outbound token encapsulates an IPv4 packet.

It prepends an outer IP header, followed by an ESP header with a SPI and a sequence number (which are obtained from a specified SA). It updates the inner IPv4 header by decrementing its ttl and updating the checksum field. The inner IPv4 is (optionally) encrypted, along with the ESP trailer and any padding needed to meet encryption block size requirements. An ICV is appended at the end.

Before packets are processed with this interface, basic data structures need to be initialized at the gxcr_ipsec level. See ipsec.h. This is the level at which IPsec parameters such as the encryption and authentication algorithms and keys are specified.

Certain parameters are determined at Security Association setup time, others are not known until a particular packet is being processed. The function ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_setup() sets up the SA-specific data. This function must be called once, at initialization time, for each Security Association.

The setup function leaves as little work as possible for the runtime function, ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet() or ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet_start(), to perform for each packet. The runtime function expects an IPv4 packet with a valid checksum. It outputs an ESP packet with an encrypted payload (padded if necessary), with a prepended Outer IP header, an ESP header containing a SPI and sequence number from the SA, and an explicit IV for the inbound side to use for decryption. It appends an ICV calculated on the packet using the specified hash algorithm. It updates the SA with an incremented sequence number to be used in the next packet processed with the SA.

See this example program for an illustration of how to use this API (along with its inbound conterpart): mica/ipsec/appc : IPsec ESP/IPv4/Tunneling Outbound and Inbound packet processing.

Data Structures

struct  ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_t
 Data structure that encapsulates general IPsec SA and operation-specific parameters. More...

Functions

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_setup (ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_t *op_sa, void *outer_ip_hdr, int outer_ip_hdr_len, int esp_hdr_len)
 Function to set up for outbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing.
int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet_start (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_t *op_sa, void *packet, int packet_len, void *dst, int dst_len)
 Function to perform outbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing on a packet.
int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_t *op_sa, void *packet, int packet_len, void *dst, int dst_len)
 Function to perform outbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing on a packet.
int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_minimum_dst_len (ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_t *op_sa, int src_len)
 Function to calculate the minimum length of the output (destination) buffer for functions: ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet_start or ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet.

Variables

gxcr_token_info_t esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound
 Token for ESP/IPv4/Tunneling Outbound packet processing.

Function Documentation

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_setup ( ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_t *  op_sa,
void *  outer_ip_hdr,
int  outer_ip_hdr_len,
int  esp_hdr_len 
)

Function to set up for outbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing.

Parameters:
op_sa - An IPsec SA initialized via gxcr_ipsec_init_sa().
outer_ip_hdr - Points to memory containing value of the IP header to prepend to outbound packet.
outer_ip_hdr_len - Length, in bytes, of the IP header to prepend to outbound packet.
esp_hdr_len - Length, in bytes, of the ESP header in outbound packets.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c, and mica/queue/app.c.

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet_start ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_t *  op_sa,
void *  packet,
int  packet_len,
void *  dst,
int  dst_len 
)

Function to perform outbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing on a packet.

This function kicks off the operation and returns immediately.

Parameters:
mica_context - An initialized MiCA context.
op_sa - An initialized operation-specific SA.
packet - The IPv4 packet to be encapsulated.
packet_len - Length, in bytes, of the packet to be processed.
dst - Destination memory for the result packet. If dst is equal to packet, the memory pointed to by packet is overwritten. The memory pointed to by packet and by dst must not overlap otherwise.
dst_len - Length, in bytes, of destination memory for the result packet.
Returns:
0 on success, error code on failure.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c.

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_t *  op_sa,
void *  packet,
int  packet_len,
void *  dst,
int  dst_len 
)

Function to perform outbound IPsec ESP IPv4 tunnel mode processing on a packet.

This function blocks until completion.

Parameters:
mica_context - An initialized MiCA context.
op_sa - An initialized operation-specific SA.
packet - The IPv4 packet to be encapsulated.
packet_len - Length, in bytes, of the packet to be processed.
dst - Destination memory for the result packet. If dst is equal to packet, the memory pointed to by packet is overwritten. The memory pointed to by packet and by dst must not overlap otherwise.
dst_len - Length, in bytes, of destination memory for the result packet.
Returns:
0 on success, error code on failure.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c.

int ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_minimum_dst_len ( ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_sa_t *  op_sa,
int  src_len 
)

Function to calculate the minimum length of the output (destination) buffer for functions: ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet_start or ipsec_esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound_process_packet.

Calls to those functions supplying an output buffer whose size, as given by the dst_len parameter, is smaller than the value returned by this function will fail.

Parameters:
op_sa An initialized operation-specific SA.
src_len Length, in bytes, of the packet to be processed.
Returns:
The minimum output data length in bytes.

Variable Documentation

gxcr_token_info_t esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound

Token for ESP/IPv4/Tunneling Outbound packet processing.

Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c, and mica/queue/app.c.
